threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think the bus will show up on time? Welcome to Hackney! Locals have their own schedules—like tea time, but with public transport.
The 36 bus driver this weekend gave everyone free rides after a passenger serenaded him with a Danny Dyer pub classic. Best night ever!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy walking his pet goat on Hermannstraße, while the scent of kebabs lingered in the air. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find an open-air cinema that plays cult classics while people lounge on the grass, complete with live DJs in between films.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to check the BART delays before brunch plans. If they still show up on the hour, they’re just visiting.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 at 6:15—driver had a random karaoke moment with a passenger, belted out 'Don’t Stop Believin'' before we took off. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell, but miss the quiet charm of Plaça del Sol at sunset, where locals unwind with a beer and tapas in hand.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're the ones trying to order a 'café con leche' in every café and still unwrapping their maps at the metro entrance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still order a flat white without emphasizing oat milk like it's a personal manifesto. Welcome to the coffee culture.
Last week on the 196, a bloke serenaded the whole bus with his karaoke version of 'Wonderwall'. Half the passengers joined in, felt like a scene from a film.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of fresh kebabs mingling with the sound of Sunday soccer matches from the park – that's when I felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club under a train bridge where the entrance is through a graffiti-covered shipping container—pure city magic!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can guess the bus route just by the sound of the engine. It’s a whole different rhythm we’ve got here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 Polk this morning. A saxophonist in plaid played 'Take Five' while a lady in a fur coat napped—only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and crowded plazas, but many miss the quiet corners where the locals sip their café amb llet, reading a newspaper.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of the ‘bicing’ stations, marveling at how the bikes don’t get stolen. Just wait a few months.
